responses.rawOutput: {"final_answer": ["Q1: N4 activates at T=2", "Q2: N5 first activates at T=1", "Q3: N3, N4, and N5 would never activate", "Q4: N4 would activate at T=1"], "confidence": 0.96, "working": ["T=0: N1 sends to N2 and N3 simultaneously; both receive at T=0.", "N2 has a 2-step delay: message reaches N4 at T=0+2 = T=2.", "N3 has a 1-step delay: message reaches N4 and N5 at T=0+1 = T=1.", "Q1: N4 needs messages from BOTH N2 (arrives T=2) and N3 (arrives T=1). Second arrival is T=2, so N4 activates at T=2.", "Q2: N5 activates immediately on any message. First message from N3 arrives at T=1, so N5 first activates at T=1. (It also receives from N4 at T=2, but first is T=1.)", "Q3: Removing N1->N3 means N3 never receives a message, so N3 never activates. N4 requires N3's message, which never comes, so N4 never activates. N5 only receives from N3 (removed) or N4 (never fires), so N5 never activates. N1 and N2 still act. Never activate: N3, N4, N5.", "Q4: With N2 delay = 0, N2's message reaches N4 at T=0. N3's message reaches N4 at T=1. Second arrival is T=1, so N4 activates at T=1."]}

final_answer: Q2: N5 first activates at T=1
Q3: N3, N4, and N5 would never activate
Q4: N4 would activate at T=1
working: T=0: N1 sends to N2 and N3 simultaneously; both receive at T=0.
N2 has a 2-step delay: message reaches N4 at T=0+2 = T=2.
N3 has a 1-step delay: message reaches N4 and N5 at T=0+1 = T=1.
Q1: N4 needs messages from BOTH N2 (arrives T=2) and N3 (arrives T=1). Second arrival is T=2, so N4 activates at T=2.
Q2: N5 activates immediately on any message. First message from N3 arrives at T=1, so N5 first activates at T=1. (It also receives from N4 at T=2, but first is T=1.)

Q3: Removing N1->N3 means N3 never receives a message, so N3 never activates. N4 requires N3's message, which never comes, so N4 never activates. N5 only receives from N3 (removed) or N4 (never fires), so N5 never activates. N1 and N2 still act. Never activate: N3, N4, N5.
Q4: With N2 delay = 0, N2's message reaches N4 at T=0. N3's message reaches N4 at T=1. Second arrival is T=1, so N4 activates at T=1.
